items.q: What board game uses properties such as Boardwalk and Park Place?
items.explanation: Monopoly was based on an earlier game called The Landlord's Game and centers on buying streets and collecting rent.

items.q: Sound travels faster in water than in air.
items.explanation: Because water particles are packed more closely than air particles, vibrations move through water more quickly.

items.q: What punctuation mark ends a direct question in English?
items.explanation: A question mark signals interrogative phrasing and is one of the most recognizable marks in English punctuation.

items.q: What is the Japanese art of paper folding called?
items.explanation: Traditional origami starts from a square sheet and creates forms through folds rather than cuts.

items.q: Which fruit is traditionally dried to make prunes?
items.explanation: Prunes are usually made from specific plum varieties that dry well while keeping a sweet flavor.

items.q: Which desert covers much of northern Africa?
items.explanation: The Sahara stretches across many countries and is one of the largest hot deserts in the world.

items.q: The treble clef is also known as the G clef.
items.explanation: The symbol curls around the second line of the staff, which marks the note G above middle C.

items.q: In baseball, how many strikes usually make a strikeout?
items.explanation: A batter who reaches three strikes is out unless a dropped third strike rule allows a play to continue.

items.q: What is the largest land animal living today?
items.explanation: African elephants can weigh several tonnes and use their trunks for breathing, grasping, and communication.

items.q: What is the name of the symbol & in English?
items.explanation: The word ampersand comes from a historical rendering of 'and per se and,' which was once recited when teaching the alphabet.

items.q: Which Dutch painter created The Night Watch?
items.explanation: The painting is famous for its dramatic use of light and its large group portrait of a civic militia.

items.q: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets in the Solar System.
items.explanation: Its slow retrograde spin means the Sun would appear to rise in the west and set in the east from Venus's surface.

items.q: In English algebraic chess notation, what letter represents the knight?
items.explanation: The king already uses K, so the knight takes N to avoid confusion in recorded moves.

items.q: Which Mughal emperor commissioned the Taj Mahal?
items.explanation: He built the mausoleum in memory of Mumtaz Mahal, and the site later became one of India's most famous landmarks.

items.q: Which writing system is used in Japanese mainly for loanwords?
items.explanation: Katakana is also often used for emphasis, scientific names, and many imported modern terms.

items.q: Which composer wrote the opera The Magic Flute?
items.explanation: The opera premiered in Vienna in 1791 and mixes comic scenes with Masonic symbolism.

items.q: The Dead Sea lies below sea level.
items.explanation: Its shoreline is one of the lowest exposed land elevations on Earth, and the lake is known for very high salinity.

items.q: What kind of rock forms when existing rock is changed by heat and pressure?
items.explanation: Slate and marble are common examples of metamorphic rock formed from older rock altered deep within Earth's crust.

items.q: What term describes animals that are mainly active at dawn and dusk?
items.explanation: Many rabbits and deer follow crepuscular patterns to balance feeding opportunities with lower predation risk.

items.q: Which poker hand ranks directly above a flush in standard poker?
items.explanation: A full house combines three cards of one rank with two of another, placing it above a flush but below four of a kind.

items.q: Which Baroque composer wrote the Goldberg Variations?
items.explanation: The work was published as the final part of Bach's Clavier-Ubung series and is built around an aria and variations.

items.q: The Rosetta Stone presents the same decree in three scripts.
items.explanation: Its parallel inscriptions in hieroglyphic, Demotic, and Greek were crucial to deciphering ancient Egyptian writing.

items.q: Which molecule carries amino acids to the ribosome during translation?
items.explanation: Each transfer RNA has an anticodon that pairs with a codon on messenger RNA while delivering a specific amino acid.

items.q: What branch of linguistics studies sentence structure?
items.explanation: Syntax focuses on how words combine into phrases and clauses, while semantics and pragmatics focus more on meaning and use.

items.q: What theorem states that every non-constant polynomial with complex coefficients has at least one complex root?
items.explanation: A key consequence is that such polynomials can be fully factored into linear factors over the complex numbers.

items.q: What economic term describes a market dominated by a small number of large sellers?
items.explanation: Airlines and telecommunications are often used as examples because a few firms can strongly influence pricing and output.

items.q: The Chandrasekhar limit describes the maximum mass of a stable white dwarf.
items.explanation: Above that threshold, electron degeneracy pressure can no longer support the star, leading to collapse or explosion.

items.q: Which desert lies mostly in northern Chile and is among the driest places on Earth?
items.explanation: Parts of the Atacama receive so little rain that some weather stations there have recorded years without measurable precipitation.

items.q: Which architect engineered the dome of Florence Cathedral?
items.explanation: Brunelleschi used a double-shell design and innovative hoisting methods to build the vast dome without traditional centering.

items.q: Which medieval work includes storytellers such as the Wife of Bath and the Pardoner?
items.explanation: Geoffrey Chaucer structured the work as a storytelling contest among pilgrims traveling toward Canterbury.
